name: Fish Pie, Modern Version of Squid Dish
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. For a 2 crust pie
2. Wrap the salmon in a piece of cheesecloth, tie securely with string and cook gently for about 30 minutes in a court bouillon made of water, white wine, salt, onion, peppercorns, and carrot.
3. Remove from the bouillon, unwrap, remove all skin, bones, and black part of the flesh. When cool enough to handle, flake it with your fingers to ensure all bones are extracted.
4. Peel the figs and add them with washed and stemmed grapes.
5. Crush the fruits together using a wooden spoon and season with marjoram, cinnamon, ginger, sugar, and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt.
6. Grate or grind the blanched almonds, moisten with rosewater, and add to the fruit mixture.
7. Stir in the flaked salmon, chopped parsley, and 4 tablespoons melted butter.
8. Beat the egg white until frothy and stir into the mixture.
9. Line a 9-inch pie dish with pastry and fill with the salmon mixture. Cover with more pastry. Trim, roll under, and crimp the edges. Make 5 or 6 generous slits in the top crust.
10. Place in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C). Bake for about 20 minutes, then increase to 400°F (200°C) and continue baking until golden brown, about 45 more minutes.
11. Serve immediately with white wine.
